name: Grade 1 Beginner Coding — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for debugging, events, and designing an interactive project. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in debugging, events, and designing an interactive project.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore debugging, events, and designing an interactive project
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record debugging, events, and designing an interactive project by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: CSTA K–12 Computer Science Standards
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Beginner Coding pathway.

units.title: Investigate: debugging, events, and designing an interactive project
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about debugging, events, and designing an interactive project?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record debugging, events, and designing an interactive project.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore debugging, events, and designing an interactive project
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record debugging, events, and designing an interactive project by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record debugging, events, and designing an interactive project. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to debugging, events, and designing an interactive project.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
